evals.prompt: Create a standalone HTML code-change explainer for a Swift/AppKit change. The reader knows Java. Explain this change: a switcher controller adds enum SwitcherSessionTrigger { case hotkey, menu }, changes handleSwitcherShortcut(trigger: SwitcherSessionTrigger = .hotkey), passes .menu from the status bar menu, installs both local and global keyDown monitors, installs local and global flagsChanged monitors only for hotkey sessions, and routes both monitor types through shared handlers. Include before/after code snippets and save the HTML output.
evals.expected_output: HTML starts with a plain problem, defines local/global monitors before relying on them, includes a before-fix timeline, and has Before/After/Why it matters.
assertions: Lesson starts by explaining the practical event-listening problem before naming architecture patterns or Java analogies.
Defines local and global monitors before relying on those terms.
Includes a before-fix timeline or numbered sequence showing how Option release was missed.
Includes a plain Before / After / Why it matters summary before the code comparison.

evals.prompt: Create a standalone HTML code-change explainer for a Swift/AppKit change. The reader knows Java. Explain this change: WindowInventoryService creates an AX app element, calls AXUIElementSetMessagingTimeout(appElement, 0.25), logs timeout setup failure but continues, reads kAXWindowsAttribute, removes guard !title.isEmpty, and relies on WindowCandidate.displayTitle returning title.isEmpty ? appName : title. Include before/after code snippets and save the HTML output.
evals.expected_output: HTML splits timeout and empty-title handling as sub-fixes, explains AX as cross-process before jargon, and points to the displayTitle fallback.
assertions: Explains AX/Accessibility as asking another app for window information before using dense AX terminology.
Separates the timeout change and empty-title change as sub-fixes or clearly distinct sections.
Includes a plain Before / After / Why it matters summary for the change or each sub-fix.
Points to the displayTitle fallback or equivalent fallback so keeping empty titles is shown to be safe.

evals.prompt: Create a standalone HTML code-change explainer for a Swift/AppKit change. The reader knows Java. Explain this change: WindowActivationService used to call AXUIElementPerformAction(kAXRaiseAction), set kAXMainAttribute and kAXFocusedAttribute, then call app.activate(options: [.activateIgnoringOtherApps, .activateAllWindows]). It now calls plain app.activate() first when the app exists, logs activation failure, still tries AX raise if the app lookup fails, logs AX raise failure, then sets main and focused attributes. Include before/after code snippets and save the HTML output.
evals.expected_output: HTML explains lifecycle ordering with a before-fix timeline and a plain-language reason before architectural terms.
assertions: Starts with the practical ordering problem in plain language before AppKit/AX architecture framing.
Includes a before-fix timeline showing how raising before app activation could be undone.
Includes a plain Before / After / Why it matters summary before the code comparison.
Explains why the new best-effort path still tries AX raise even if app lookup or activation fails.
